---
title: "Webhooks"
description: "Receive real-time event notifications for order and shipment state changes."
---

## Overview

Configure a webhook endpoint to receive HTTP POST notifications when orders and
shipments transition through key lifecycle states. UrbnBox signs every delivery
with an HMAC-SHA256 signature so you can verify authenticity before processing.

## Configure a webhook

`POST /api/vendor/v1/webhooks`

```json
{
  "url": "https://your-backend.example.com/urbnbox/events",
  "events": ["order.confirmed", "order.delivered", "shipment.collected"]
}
```

The response includes a `secret` field. Store this value securely — it is shown
only once and is used to verify the HMAC signature on incoming events.

## Available events

| Event | Fired when |
| --- | --- |
| `order.confirmed` | A client confirms an order on the public page |
| `order.assigned` | An admin assigns a rider to the order |
| `order.dispatched` | The order is marked as dispatched |
| `order.delivered` | The order is marked as delivered |
| `order.cancelled` | The order is cancelled at any stage |
| `shipment.deposited` | Sender deposits the parcel at the origin locker |
| `shipment.in_transit` | Rider picks up the parcel from the origin locker |
| `shipment.arrived` | Parcel arrives at the destination locker |
| `shipment.collected` | Recipient collects the parcel |

## Verifying the signature

Each delivery includes an `X-UrbnBox-Signature` header containing a
HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of the raw request body, keyed with your webhook secret.

```python
import hmac, hashlib

def verify(secret: str, payload: bytes, signature: str) -> bool:
    expected = hmac.new(
        secret.encode(), payload, hashlib.sha256
    ).hexdigest()
    return hmac.compare_digest(expected, signature)
```

Reject any delivery where the signature does not match. Never process an event
before verifying its signature.

## Retry policy

UrbnBox retries failed deliveries (non-2xx or timeout) with exponential backoff
up to 5 attempts over 24 hours. Respond with HTTP 200 as quickly as possible —
queue heavy processing for a background job.

## List and delete webhooks

`GET /api/vendor/v1/webhooks` — returns all configured webhooks for the vendor.

`DELETE /api/vendor/v1/webhooks/{id}` — removes a webhook endpoint.
